agenda_items.title: High-Risk Knowledge Transfer
agenda_items.description: 2 files with high knowledge loss risk
discussion_points: Review each high-risk file and explain key concepts
Discuss why these files are critical to the system
Identify potential knowledge gaps
Document any undocumented assumptions or decisions

agenda_items.title: Critical Subsystem Knowledge
discussion_points: Review Critical Subsystem Knowledge in detail
Discuss key concepts and implementation details
Identify knowledge gaps and documentation needs
Document important decisions and rationale

agenda_items.title: Known Failure Scenarios & Workarounds
discussion_points: Review known failure scenarios and their causes
Discuss workarounds and temporary fixes
Explain recovery procedures
Document lessons learned from past incidents

agenda_items.title: Architecture & Design Decisions
discussion_points: Explain the rationale behind key architecture decisions
Discuss trade-offs and alternatives considered
Document any constraints or assumptions
Review future scalability or maintenance considerations

agenda_items.title: Knowledge Hotspot Areas
discussion_points: Review Knowledge Hotspot Areas in detail
Discuss key concepts and implementation details
Identify knowledge gaps and documentation needs
Document important decisions and rationale

questions.question: What unique insights do you have about the Makefile that are not documented?
questions.what_it_reveals: Unwritten rules or optimizations in the Makefile that could affect build processes.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer reveals undocumented dependencies or processes.

questions.question: Can you identify any specific areas in the github.sh script that are prone to failure during deployment?
questions.what_it_reveals: Potential points of failure that could disrupt the CI/CD pipeline.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer highlights critical failure points.

questions.question: What are the key interactions between the Makefile and other project components that are not immediately obvious?
questions.what_it_reveals: Hidden dependencies that could lead to issues if not understood.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer suggests complex interactions with other files.

questions.question: What troubleshooting steps do you take when the Makefile fails to execute as expected?
questions.what_it_reveals: Expert troubleshooting methods that may not be documented.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer includes specific techniques or tools used.

questions.question: What are the most common mistakes new team members make when using github.sh?
questions.what_it_reveals: Common pitfalls that could lead to inefficiencies or errors.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer provides a list of mistakes and their consequences.

questions.question: What undocumented features or functionalities exist within the Makefile that you rely on?
questions.what_it_reveals: Critical features that could be overlooked by others.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer points out features that are not in the documentation.

questions.question: What specific configurations in github.sh should be monitored to prevent issues after your departure?
questions.what_it_reveals: Configurations that require special attention to maintain stability.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer includes configurations that are prone to change.

questions.question: What are the key performance metrics you track related to the Makefile and github.sh?
questions.what_it_reveals: Metrics that indicate the health and performance of the build and deployment process.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer includes specific metrics that are critical for success.

questions.question: What unique insights do you have about the critical subsystems that are not documented in our current manuals?
questions.what_it_reveals: Unwritten knowledge that could impact system functionality.
questions.follow_up_if: If the answer reveals undocumented processes or configurations.

questions.question: Can you identify any specific dependencies between subsystems that could lead to failures if not properly managed?
questions.what_it_reveals: Potential points of failure that may not be obvious to others.
questions.follow_up_if: If they mention critical dependencies that are not documented.

questions.question: What are the most common issues you encounter with these subsystems, and how do you resolve them?
questions.what_it_reveals: Common pitfalls and troubleshooting methods that are not formally recorded.
questions.follow_up_if: If they provide specific examples of issues and solutions.

questions.question: What tools or scripts do you use to monitor or maintain these subsystems that others might not be aware of?
questions.what_it_reveals: Specialized tools that are essential for system health but not widely known.
questions.follow_up_if: If they mention tools that are not included in the standard toolkit.

questions.question: Are there any undocumented protocols or procedures you follow that are critical for subsystem operations?
questions.what_it_reveals: Essential operational procedures that lack formal documentation.
questions.follow_up_if: If they describe any procedures that are not in the existing documentation.

questions.question: What would you say are the top three risks associated with these subsystems if you were to leave?
questions.what_it_reveals: Critical risks that could arise from the loss of their expertise.
questions.follow_up_if: If they identify specific risks that are not currently mitigated.

questions.question: What are the key performance indicators you track for these subsystems, and why are they important?
questions.what_it_reveals: Metrics that are crucial for subsystem health that may not be monitored by others.
questions.follow_up_if: If they mention KPIs that are not part of regular reporting.

questions.question: Can you describe a situation where your intervention prevented a major failure in the subsystems?
questions.what_it_reveals: Real-life examples of their impact on system reliability.
questions.follow_up_if: If they recount a specific incident that highlights their unique contributions.

questions.question: Can you describe specific failure scenarios you've encountered that are not documented in our current knowledge base?
questions.what_it_reveals: Unwritten insights into failure scenarios that could impact operations.
questions.follow_up_if: If they mention a scenario, ask for detailed steps taken to resolve it.

questions.question: What workarounds have you developed for issues that are known but not widely communicated?
questions.what_it_reveals: Alternative solutions that can prevent downtime or inefficiencies.
questions.follow_up_if: If they provide a workaround, ask for any potential downsides or limitations.

questions.question: Are there any specific tools or scripts you use that others might not be aware of?
questions.what_it_reveals: Critical tools that are essential for managing failure scenarios.
questions.follow_up_if: If they mention a tool, ask how it integrates with current systems.

questions.question: What common assumptions do you find others make that could lead to failures?
questions.what_it_reveals: Misconceptions that could lead to operational risks if not addressed.
questions.follow_up_if: If they identify an assumption, explore how to mitigate the associated risks.

questions.question: What are the key indicators you monitor to anticipate potential failures?
questions.what_it_reveals: Proactive measures that can be taken to avoid failures.
questions.follow_up_if: If they list indicators, ask how they determine when to act on them.

questions.question: Can you share any lessons learned from past failures that are not documented?
questions.what_it_reveals: Insights that can inform future decision-making and prevent recurrence.
questions.follow_up_if: If they share a lesson, ask how it was applied in practice.

questions.question: What processes do you think are most vulnerable to failure after your departure?
questions.what_it_reveals: Identifies areas that need immediate attention to ensure continuity.
questions.follow_up_if: If they identify a process, ask what specific actions should be taken to strengthen it.

questions.question: What documentation or training materials do you believe need to be created or updated before your exit?
questions.what_it_reveals: Identifies gaps in documentation that could hinder operations.
questions.follow_up_if: If they suggest materials, ask for specific content that should be included.

discussion_order.title: High-Risk Knowledge Transfer
discussion_order.rationale: First topic due to critical priority and high risk score

discussion_order.title: Critical Subsystem Knowledge
discussion_order.rationale: Positioned based on priority (high) and risk assessment

discussion_order.title: Known Failure Scenarios & Workarounds
discussion_order.rationale: Positioned based on priority (high) and risk assessment

discussion_order.title: Architecture & Design Decisions
discussion_order.rationale: Positioned based on priority (high) and risk assessment

discussion_order.title: Knowledge Hotspot Areas
discussion_order.rationale: Positioned based on priority (medium) and risk assessment

suggested_discussion_order.order_rationale: Topics ordered by priority (critical > high > medium > low) and risk score

time_estimates.title: High-Risk Knowledge Transfer
factors: High risk score indicates need for thorough discussion

time_estimates.title: Critical Subsystem Knowledge
factors: High risk score indicates need for thorough discussion

time_estimates.title: Known Failure Scenarios & Workarounds
factors: Operational topics typically require more detailed explanation
High risk score indicates need for thorough discussion

high_risk_knowledge.justification: This topic has critical priority with a risk score of 0.90. High-risk knowledge files represent critical system components where knowledge loss could significantly impact operations.
high_risk_knowledge.impact_if_missed: Critical impact: System operations may be disrupted, knowledge may be permanently lost, and recovery could be difficult.
high_risk_knowledge.recommendation: Must be discussed in Final Call. Consider scheduling additional follow-up sessions if needed.

critical_subsystems.justification: This topic has high priority with a risk score of 0.85. This topic requires discussion to ensure knowledge continuity.
critical_subsystems.impact_if_missed: High impact: Significant knowledge gaps may develop, requiring extensive time to recover understanding.
critical_subsystems.recommendation: Strongly recommended for Final Call discussion. Document all key points.

failure_scenarios.justification: This topic has high priority with a risk score of 0.85. This topic requires discussion to ensure knowledge continuity.
failure_scenarios.impact_if_missed: High impact: Significant knowledge gaps may develop, requiring extensive time to recover understanding.
failure_scenarios.recommendation: Strongly recommended for Final Call discussion. Document all key points.

architecture_decisions.justification: This topic has high priority with a risk score of 0.80. This topic requires discussion to ensure knowledge continuity.
architecture_decisions.impact_if_missed: High impact: Significant knowledge gaps may develop, requiring extensive time to recover understanding.
architecture_decisions.recommendation: Strongly recommended for Final Call discussion. Document all key points.

knowledge_hotspots.justification: This topic has medium priority with a risk score of 0.70. This topic requires discussion to ensure knowledge continuity.
knowledge_hotspots.impact_if_missed: Medium impact: Knowledge gaps may develop, but recovery is possible with additional effort.
